text: Capstone thesis: mathematics' global structure is recoverable from formal proofs with NO supervision - Leiden clustering of Mathlib's dependency graph reproduces named domains (a-0001), and an in-degree-filtered cross-cluster premise criterion detects operational bridge theorems (a-0007) that align with historically celebrated connections (a-0008). This operationalizes one notion of mathematical interestingness (bridges) and identifies SetTheory as the integrative hub (a-0010). The central open problem is the gap between operational bridges (shared premises, detected) and conceptual bridges (shared proof structure, undetected; a-0009). Bridge-aware proving (a-0011) is the proposed but untested payoff.

comments.placeholders: The paper's 'compressibility' framing leans on a placeholder reference (compression2024, bibliographic details = TODO); authors/affiliations are also placeholders. Reasoning content is unaffected.
comments.caveat: CAVEAT (e-0057/q-0021): the 'identifies SetTheory as the integrative hub (a-0010)' clause is down-scoped -- a-0010 is superseded by a-0053. Under data hygiene (non-math nodes removed) SetTheory falls to CN rank 3 behind 'Data' infrastructure clusters; it remains a top-3 integrative cluster but is not the single dominant hub. The capstone's core (a-0001 unsupervised domain recovery + a-0007 depth-filtered operational bridges) is unaffected; bridge MEMBERSHIP remains resolution-relative (a-0048/a-0051/a-0052).
comments.caveat2: CAVEAT (e-0058/q-0020): the operational-bridge clause (a-0007) is down-scoped by a-0055 -- the depth-filtered 34-bridge set is NOT a clustering-independent object (permutation null recovers ~98%, held-out clustering <7%, external module partition precision 0.005). The detector certifies WITHIN-partition premise specificity, not a partition-invariant set of cross-domain bridges. Unsupervised domain RECOVERY (a-0001) is unaffected.
